summary: Broad permuter-driven matching PR across fighter, stage, item, game-mode, library, menu, camera, and baselib files. The author stated the intent was to run the permuter on many functions and commit results that reached 100%. The bot report records +0.32% matched code, +12608 bytes, 45 new matches, 5 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 unmatched-item regression in lbcardnew.

what_changed: 31 files changed with 267 added lines and 132 deleted lines.
The PR body says the author ran the permuter on many functions and planned to commit results that reached 100%.
The decomp-dev bot report for GALE01 records matched code increasing to 65.73%, a +0.32% delta and +12608 bytes.

The bot report lists 45 new matches, including functions in itsamusgrapple, gm_16AE, ftPp_SpecialS, lbcardnew, itseakchain, lb_0192, vi1201v2, ftKb_Init, gm_1601, mndatadel, groldyoshi, hsd_3AA7, camera, grkongo, itlinkboomerang, itmewtwoshadowball, sobjlib, lbsnap, grmutecity, grfourside, and grgreatbay.
The bot report lists 5 improvements in unmatched items: three particle functions, gm_18A5 fn_801981A0, and ftCo_Guard .sdata2.
The bot report lists 1 unmatched-item regression: main/melee/lb/lbcardnew lb_8001BC18, -4 bytes, 96.62% to 95.28%.

Diff excerpt shows many codegen-shaping edits: extra temporaries named new_var or fp2, declaration hoisting/reordering, PAD_STACK, direct user_data access versus GET_* macros, added inline wrappers, explicit casts, and expression splitting.

smart_moves.text: The PR relied on narrow bot-reported match evidence for many small function closures rather than claiming broad subsystem cleanup.
The bot report exposed both gains and a regression, which makes the tradeoff visible for curator/reviewer follow-up.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Permuter output can close many near-matching functions by changing temporary lifetimes, declaration order, casts, macro/accessor choice, and expression grouping, but the resulting source may be mechanically shaped rather than authored-style.
A large batch of 100% matches can still include localized regressions; record bot/regression output per affected unit rather than treating the PR as uniformly positive.

Accessor and macro choice can affect matching: this PR contains both direct user_data replacements and GET_FIGHTER/GET_GROUND-related changes, so match-driven accessor changes need local verification rather than a blanket rule.

naming_conventions.pattern: Mechanical temporary names such as new_var, fp2, unused1, and state-pointer expression rewrites appear in the diff.
naming_conventions.note: These names are evidence of permuter-shaped matching work, not a recommended naming convention.

naming_conventions.pattern: Existing address-style names such as fn_80122B54, fn_80165E7C, fn_801981A0, and grOldYoshi_8020EFCC were preserved.
naming_conventions.note: No evidence in the slice supports new semantic naming standards.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split a complex expression into an explicit temporary before a call, e.g. Camera_8002FEEC computes new_var before sqrtf__Ff.

Change object-user-data access shape, including GET_FIGHTER to HSD_GObjGetUserData in Kirby hat functions, direct user_data access in some ground functions, and direct user_data to GET_FIGHTER in ftCo_Guard.
Hoist declarations and widen temporary lifetimes, as in grKongo_801D7700 and grGreatBay_801F62F8.
Introduce PAD_STACK in selected functions such as fn_80122B54 and fn_801FC9AC.
Add local inline wrappers to alter call/access shape, e.g. grFlatzone_80216F48_inline and grOldYoshi_8020EFCC_inline.
Use explicit casts or constants to affect codegen, such as casting arg1 to long long in gm_16AE and using 0L in ftPp_SpecialS.
Use comma-expression or assignment-in-condition forms to steer evaluation/codegen, e.g. grKongo dz computation and grMuteCity comparison.

notes: Changed-file list and PR metadata are complete in the loaded context.
Raw diff is marked truncated, so not every hunk could be inspected directly.
No human review comments are present in the loaded slice; review feedback is limited to the bot report.
The bot report supports match/improvement/regression claims, but no local build logs, objdiff details, or reviewer acceptance rationale beyond merge metadata are included.

Some diff shapes conflict with current decomp standards favoring authored-style source and narrow evidence for PAD_STACK/dummy temporaries; this intake records them as tactics observed in the PR, not accepted style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2440 permuter batch produced many small 100% matches plus one regression
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2440, a permuter batch across 31 files produced a bot-reported +0.32% matched-code increase, +12608 bytes, and 45 new matches, while also introducing one unmatched-item regression in lbcardnew. Use as a case study for preserving per-unit regression evidence in broad matching PRs.

source_update_candidates.title: Document permuter-batch review risk: matches can be mechanically shaped and still regress a unit
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2440 is evidence that permuter batches may close many functions while introducing mechanical temporaries, PAD_STACK, inline wrappers, and at least one reported regression. Consider adding this as an example under matching tactics or verification ledger standards, emphasizing that such tactics require narrow verifier evidence and regression tracking.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already cover tactic evidence and regression ledgers, but this PR offers a concrete historical example for curator consideration.

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2440 touched a broad set of matching-sensitive paths
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2440 changed 31 paths across src/melee and src/sysdolphin/baselib, with notable match report entries for ftKb_Init, ftPp_SpecialS, gm_16AE, gm_1601, gm_18A5, camera, grkongo, groldyoshi, lbcardnew, particle, and several item files.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future path-scoped searches for historical permuter work, but should remain curator-reviewed because the diff was truncated in the loaded slice.

rejection_notes: Do not promote new_var, fp2, unused1, PAD_STACK, comma expressions, or wrapper inlines as accepted style based on this PR alone; the PR evidence only shows they were used in a permuter-driven match batch.
Do not infer reviewer approval of each tactic; there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
Do not claim all 31 changed files fully matched; the bot report lists some improvements and one regression, and the raw diff is truncated.
Do not treat direct user_data access or GET_FIGHTER/HSD_GObjGetUserData changes as a global accessor rule; the PR contains both directions depending on function.
